summary: Roblox/Discord/browser credential stealer with persistence, exfiltrating to Discord webhook on import
details: On `import spaysdata`, the package's __init__.py invokes main_entry() inside a bare try/except, which executes a credential-stealer payload entirely unrelated to its advertised purpose (the PKG-INFO advertises a Roblox DataStore library; no DataStore code is present). The payload performs three distinct attacks against the installer's Windows machine: (1) reads %USERPROFILE%/AppData/Local/Roblox/LocalStorage/robloxcookies.dat, DPAPI-decrypts it, and POSTs the Roblox session cookies to a hardcoded Discord webhook (spaysdata/main.py:16,47); (2) kills Discord and walks the Local Storage leveldb directories for Discord, Chrome, Edge, Brave, Opera, Yandex, Vivaldi, and Firefox profiles, extracting and DPAPI-decrypting auth tokens and posting each working token plus user info to the same webhook (main.py:71); (3) copies itself to %APPDATA%/MySystemUtility and writes an HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run entry named 'MyPythonAutostartApp' for persistence across logins. The console window is hidden via ctypes ShowWindow(0) and all errors are silently swallowed; in-source comments (Ukrainian) explicitly state the victim must not see anything. The package name targets Roblox developers as a lure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
